name: stale-ltads-state
question: Is ltads/sessions/current-session.md inconsistent with the active-sessions.json registry (state ACTIVE but registry says closed/expired/missing/PID-dead)?

details.registry_pid_alive: boolean|null (true/false when status=active and PID is non-null; null otherwise)

schema.briefing: string (empty when stale=false; one-liner suitable for ORACLE BRIEFING when stale=true)

fallback: Read ltads/sessions/current-session.md and grep for 'Status:' and 'Session:'. Read .claude/collaboration/active-sessions.json and look for the entry whose .id == that session id. If state Status is ACTIVE/PAUSED/IN_PROGRESS but the registry entry is in closed[] or absent or its PID is not alive, that's a stale-state inconsistency.

provenance.note: Third leg of CSI Phase 1.6's three-leg session-end cleanup defense. CSI-12 (SessionEnd hook) is proactive; CSI-13 (GC coupling) is reactive at next-session-start; CSI-14 is reflective -- it surfaces inconsistencies that slipped through both. Briefing-line emission gracefully absent when state is consistent.
